title: University
description:
I graduated from the Singapore University of Social Sciences, majoring in Business Analytics with a Minor in Information Technology.

My academic journey focused on Machine Learning and Data Visualisation, where I built a strong foundation in areas such as predictive modelling, clustering and association analysis, deep learning, reinforcement learning, and natural language processing.

Beyond theory, my studies emphasized practical application—bridging data-driven insights with real-world implementation through hands-on projects and technical development.
details: Finalist — SUSS Business Cohort FYP: Contemporary Business Strategy Competition
Teaching Assistant for Selenium Web Scraping

subSections.title: Final Year Project
subSections.cardDescription: Sephora Skincare Analytics using NLP and Clustering

content.text:
Awarded Grade: A-

Abstract:

This paper leveraged text mining techniques, including sentiment analysis and topic modeling on skincare product reviews from Sephora to uncover the common complaints about specific brands and products.

Algorithms like Valance Aware Dictionary and Sentiment Reasoner (VADER) and Fine-Tuned DistilBERT for sentiment analysis, and Term-Frequency Inverse Document Frequency (TF-IDF) and Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) were compared to determine the most suitable model for the dataset.

Through the analysis of 22 top and bottom-performing skincare brands, specific product strengths and weaknesses were identified, guiding future investigations into specific product ingredients responsible for the shortcomings.

Additionally, K-Means clustering was performed on products to address the low-sales product groups, facilitating targeted marketing strategies.

Despite uncovering insights for the skincare brands, generalizing the findings and solutions remains a challenge due to the dominant presence of American and Asian reviews. Future studies could explore beyond these regions to enhance the robustness of the findings on a global scale, ultimately developing effective well-received skincare product formulations universally.

content.text: I developed a strong foundation in Machine Learning and Deep Learning, focusing on building predictive models, analyzing complex datasets, and applying techniques such as clustering, classification, and neural networks. Alongside this, I strengthened my SQL skills to efficiently query, manage, and transform data, enabling end-to-end data-driven solutions from extraction to model deployment. Here are the toy projects I worked on during my undergraduate studies.

mlProjects.title: Mobile Price Classification
mlProjects.description: This is a classic ML project for learning the fundamentals, applying classification techniques like Logistic Regression against a Naive Bayes model, and investigating whether scaling affects the results.

mlProjects.title: Wine Clustering
mlProjects.description: The wine dataset is obtained from the sklearn.datasets library. This project aimed to teach me to understand how to perform clustering with Python and its corresponding evaluation metrics.

mlProjects.title: Diabetes Prediction
mlProjects.description: Moving into contextual understanding, this project compares Support Vector Machines and Decision Trees on a diabetes dataset. It further emphasizes the importance of avoiding false negatives in a medical context.

mlProjects.title: Galaxy Stars Classification
mlProjects.description: An interesting toy project that engaged the techniques of ensemble models, proposing the optimal depth and number of trees in a random forest model to predict a star type.

dlProjects.title: Simple Neural Network Classifier
dlProjects.description: This project is my first exposure to Neural Networks, aiming to identify the gender of a wrestler given the other parameters, prepared through data encoding. I designed a simple neural network classifier with 1 hidden layer and compared it to a Naive Bayes classifier to facilitate my understanding of the topic.

dlProjects.title: Image Classification with CNN
dlProjects.description: I have always wondered how a computer recognizes images. Surprisingly, it does relate to my previous course of study (motion graphics). These images are recognized using pixel values of RGB color channels, forming an array of information, taking values between 0-255 per pixel. This project aims to identify whether an image is a cat or a rabbit to enhance my learning on CNNs.

dlProjects.title: LSTM Stock Price Prediction
dlProjects.description: It is interesting how the concept of ARIMA models is similar to Recurrent Neural Networks, using a series of sequential data to make forecasts. However, due to the limitation of RNN memory capacity to remember previous data iterations, I used LSTM instead to predict the price of one Singapore stock exchange data.

dlProjects.title: Reinforcement Q-Learning Snake Game
dlProjects.description: Developed an AI agent that learns to play the classic Snake game autonomously using Reinforcement Learning. I implemented Deep Q-Networks (DQN) using PyTorch, allowing the agent to continuously maximize its score through reward-based exploration and exploitation.

dataProcessing.title: Data Processing & SQL
dataProcessing.caption: Consistent practice on complex queries and data manipulation.

reportShowcase.title: Data Programming Assignment
reportShowcase.description: This assignment demonstrates strong proficiency in **data programming and data manipulation using Python**. It focuses on the use of libraries such as **Pandas** and **NetworkX**, alongside database integration with **SQLite** to perform extract, transform, and load (ETL) operations, as well as complex data calculations. The work highlights the ability to handle real-world data workflows and build efficient data pipelines. I achieved a score of **99/100**, reflecting a high level of technical accuracy and implementation quality.

title: Infineon Technologies Product Master Data Intern
description: A 1-year work-study immersion focusing on Digitalization, workflow automation, and developing AI/ML solutions for the global end-to-end supply chain.

content.text:
I did my 1 year work-study program with Infineon Technologies as a Digitalization intern for the Product Master Data Department and it was nothing short of a meaningful and enriching experience. 

During my time, I proposed an AI/ML project aiming to improve the operational workflow process of the department after learning about Infineon's end-to-end supply chain and a hands-on experience with its systems. Contributing to the project, I have conducted meetings and communicated my ideas with various stakeholders such as senior management, IT colleagues, and data scientists. During the weekly team meetings, I played the role of a technical expert, understanding the business requirements shared by my colleagues and translating them into actionable results through Python programming. The technicality involved creating an ETL pipeline using the **Pandas** library and performing ML operations on the dataset for supply chain route predictions. Apart from the technicalities, I had the opportunity to participate in various events conducted by Infineon and network with many brilliant individuals. The most memorable event would be the Oktobertech where emerging technologies of the semiconductor industry are showcased through robotics, engineering, and machine learning. Finally, my colleagues are very friendly and supportive, providing me with the warmth and comfort that was very much appreciated. This department feels like a big family and I am very thankful for the opportunity to be part of it!

title: UOB – Technology Development Program (Software Track)
description: A focused journey in software engineering and applied AI within a progressive banking environment, driving impactful enterprise automation.

content.text:
I joined UOB through the Technology Development Program (Software Track), where I underwent a three-month structured training in collaboration with Ngee Ann Polytechnic. The program equipped us with foundational technical and domain knowledge to prepare for real-world deployments. It culminated in a capstone project, where my team and I achieved second place with our solution on leveraging predictive machine learning techniques to detect fraudulent credit card transactions. 

For my first assignment, I was placed in a Governance role, where I supported the coordination of requirements from senior management and stakeholders. During this period, I had the opportunity to deliver my first presentation to the Head of Group Technology and Operations. While initially challenging, the experience pushed me out of my comfort zone and helped me build confidence in both communication and presentation—skills that later proved valuable when I emceed the Group Banking Technology 2025 Townhall. 

I later transitioned to the Digital Banking space, where I was tasked with improving internal workflow processes. This marked a shift towards more hands-on technical work, where I focused on building automation tools and exploring AI-driven solutions to enhance efficiency and reduce manual effort within the team.

bulletPoints.title: Projects & Achievements
items: **Built an end-to-end RAG pipeline for PDF parsing**, parsing PDFs, chunking them into smaller pieces, utilizing embeddings, vector databases, and querying with LLM.
**Automated JUnit5 test generation** using Python and LLMs for legacy bank applications.
**Built a regex-based refactoring tool** engineered to detect unclosed mocked static references across large JUnit5 repositories.
**Developed a excel report automation platform** leveraging **Antigravity** with Springboot, Flask, SQL and Pandas.

title: Personal Projects
description: During my free time, I like to explore emerging technologies and work on personal projects to upskill and equip myself with the necessary expertise to stay relevant! Here are some projects focused on Machine Learning and AI for you to explore!

items.title: AI Travel Planner
items.description: This is a **PROTOTYPE** Travel Itinerary Planner built using Google Antigravity. I built my travel itinerary app because I've experienced how time-consuming and fragmented trip planning can be. From searching for destinations and comparing options to organizing everything into a coherent schedule, the process often involves jumping between multiple platforms and still feeling uncertain about the final plan. I envisioned a better experience, one where planning is streamlined, personalized, and automated. By leveraging AI, I want to create a tool that makes travel planning feel effortless rather than overwhelming. This project is a testament to the power of AI-assisted development, allowing for rapid prototyping and the creation of sophisticated applications with minimal boilerplate code. It demonstrates the potential of AI tools to enhance developer productivity and creativity.

items.title: Brain Tumor Detection with YOLOv10
items.description: A deep learning project focused on early brain tumor detection using YOLOv10. This project explores advanced computer vision techniques to identify tumors in MRI scans, aiming to improve diagnostic accuracy and speed.
